feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Router brands could be misleading you with that Wi-Fi 7 label
entry.content: Wi-Fi standards remain as confusing as ever.

entry.title: 2 High-Yield Dividend Stocks Just Got Kicked Out of the S&P 500. Is Either a Buy Now?
entry.content: 2 High-Yield Dividend Stocks Just Got Kicked Out of the S&P 500. Is Either a Buy Now?

entry.title: Mourners chant for revenge as dayslong funeral of Iran's slain leader begins
entry.content: The flag-draped casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was put on display in Tehran with millions expected to attend his dayslong funeral.

entry.title: 3 AI Data Center Power Stocks to Buy in July
entry.content: 3 AI Data Center Power Stocks to Buy in July

entry.title: July 4th celebrations mark America's 250th birthday. Follow live updates.
entry.content: Americans are celebrating 250 years of independence this Fourth of July with events including the largest fireworks display in history.

entry.title: 1 symptom leads to dad's cancer diagnosis: "I was like, 'Wait, what?'"
entry.content: Eric Dillon thought the pain in his shoulder was a minor injury. It took two years to get the real answer.

entry.title: Family-owned company prepares to put on the largest fireworks display in history
entry.content: When the sun goes down on Independence Day, the skies of Washington, D.C., are expected to fill with a record-setting 850,000 individual fireworks for a 40-minute spectacle like no one has seen before.

entry.title: Midjourney wants the Hollywood studios that sued it to show the court how they use AI
entry.content: Midjourney is asking the court to get Disney, Warner Bros. and Universal to submit information on their AI use.

entry.title: Trump warns of "communist menace" in speech at Mount Rushmore
entry.content: The president kicked off America's 250th anniversary celebrations with a speech at Mount Rushmore where he warned of a resurgence in communism.

entry.title: 6 presidential speeches that shaped American history
entry.content: These six presidential speeches are some that have most reverberated through the ages, and whose impacts are still felt today.

entry.title: 3 dead and others rescued after boat capsizes on Wisconsin lake amid storms
entry.content: Three people died and seven others were rescued after a boat capsized on a Wisconsin lake during a strong storm

entry.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entry.content: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entry.title: 36-54 Chart
entry.content: Then again, they might not be Giants.

entry.title: Draymond Green believes the Los Angeles Lakers will regret letting LeBron James go
entry.content: The Lakers have parted ways with LeBron James after eight years, and Draymond Green believes it's a move they will regret.

entry.title: Trent Grisham makes smashing return to Yankees lineup, losing streak ends
entry.content: The Bombers put a seven-game skid to bed, and Grisham played a key role in his first game back.

entry.title: Former Ohio State hockey goalie signed to 3-year contract extension
entry.content: Former Ohio State goalie Jakub Dobes was signed to a three-year contact extesdion by the Montreal Candiens.

entry.title: Yankees 5, Twins 2: When it rains, it snores
entry.content: Post rain delay, the Twins quickly surrender the lead and never really give you hope again; you know this story already.

entry.title: Matchday: Oakland Roots SC at New Mexico United
entry.content: Saturday When: 7:25 p.m. Where: Isotopes Park, 1601 Avenida Cesar Chavez SE TV/streaming/radio: New Mexico 50/ESPN+/FM-101.7 Records: Oakland Roots SC (5-3-6), New Mexico United (5-4-3) The series: Oakland leads 4-3-3. And we’re back. Well, New Mexico United is anyway. After a two-week break, the club returns to USL Championship play on Saturday against Oakland Roots SC. And that means things ...

entry.title: Mountain West launches subscription-based streaming platform for game, team-specific content
entry.content: The Mountain West conference this week launched its new "direct-to-consumer" digital subscriptions platform called MW+. While the days of getting all the conference-hosted games and events for free online at the Mountain West's website are over, the new app-based subscription comes with higher production standards for all conference members and revenue generating options for the specific ...

entry.title: Knicks prepared to battle Lakers for $30 million Nuggets veteran center
entry.content: The New York Knicks may pursue a 6-foot-11 Denver Nuggets big man.

entry.title: What World Cup? Italians Are Having the Worst Summer Ever.
entry.content: While the rest of the world revels in the tournament, Italians are purposefully ignoring it.

entry.title: China Is Devastating the Last Stronghold of German Industry
entry.content: Midsize companies that employ millions of workers are now shedding jobs and relocating overseas to cut costs.

entry.title: The co-owner of lumber mill has died of injuries from the mill fire and explosion
entry.content: The co-owner of a Maine lumber mill has died from injuries he sustained during a fire and explosion at the facility, bringing to three the number of fatalities

entry.title: Chess federation suspends former world champion who accused Naroditsky of cheating
entry.content: The international governing body for chess has suspended former world champion Vladimir Kramnik for at least a year

entry.title: High-Earner Families Are Ditching Traditional Schools for Life Skills and AI
entry.content: People who can afford to send their grade-school kids anywhere are choosing new alternative education.

entry.title: How a Mayor Pulled Off a Bloodless Coup to Become the U.K.’s Leader in Waiting
entry.content: Andy Burnham happened to offer the right political brand at a time when voters dislike most lawmakers.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Eat chocolate to have a sweeter life.
Lucky Numbers: 6, 94, 57, 38, 39, 96

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Put old newspaper at the bottom of your trash bin to absorb moisture.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Thirty-five percent of the people who use personal ads for dating are already married.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Aristotle
entry.content: “Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.” — Aristotle

entry.title: Trump Heads to Mount Rushmore, Where He Wants to See His Face
entry.content: Geotechnical surveys have ruled out adding to the monument that is hosting the president on the eve of America’s 250th birthday.

entry.title: He Guarded His Boss’s Home. Now Trump Wants Him to Command ICE.
entry.content: Lance Schroyer has ties with Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin—but little federal government experience.

entry.title: Tibet-Born Man Dies Outside U.N. After Appearing to Set Himself on Fire
entry.content: The incident occurred a day after China put in force a law viewed as the culmination of a long-running campaign to assimilate the country’s ethnic minorities.

entry.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
entry.content: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

entry.title: Police investigate a shopping mall shooting that left 2 dead in suburban Detroit
entry.content: A shooting altercation between two groups of young people at a shopping mall in Dearborn, Michigan, left two people dead and a third injured Friday, police said.

entry.title: Trump announces pardons for pollution violators prosecuted for "fixing their car"
entry.content: CBS News previously reported President Trump was weighing pardons of a slate of people convicted of emissions and clean air-related violations.

entry.title: White Sox trade idea sends two prospects to Giants for 3.39 ERA former Cy Young winner
entry.content: This trade would be a huge help for the White Sox rotation in 2026.

entry.title: Polymarket Promo Code TSNEWS: Get $50 Prediction Bonus for Argentina Knockout Match
entry.content: Register using the Polymarket promo code TSNEWS and make a $20 deposit to claim a $50 bonus for the Argentina match.

entry.title: Australia vs. Egypt live score, result, highlights from 2026 World Cup as Round of 32 clash moves into extra time
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights for Australia vs. Egypt in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Follow for complete results from the Round of 32 match in Dallas.

entry.title: Broncos' newest offensive weapon predicted to hit massive numbers in 2026
entry.content: Jaylen Waddle is expected to be an immediate success for the Denver Broncos in 2026.

entry.title: Argentina vs. Cape Verde live score, result, highlights from 2026 World Cup knockout game
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights for Argentina vs. Cape Verde in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Follow for complete results from the Round of 32 match in Dallas.

entry.title: Why the Green Bay Packers should sign Austin Ekeler before the 2026 NFL season
entry.content: The Green Bay Packers need to add more depth and firepower to their running back room, which is why the team should sign former Los Angeles Chargers and Washington Commanders starting running back Austin Ekeler.

entry.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce: Revisit the roots of their love story ahead of MSG wedding bash
entry.content: Revisit the roots of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's whirlwind romance turned generational love story, from the Kelce brothers' 'New Heights' podcast to a romantic garden engagement.

entry.title: Sophie Cunningham celebrates Fourth of July with swimsuit photo after viral week
entry.content: Cunningham has been popular this week, but her swimsuit shot helped her popularity.

entry.title: 76ers' Jaylen Brown fires shot at MLB, NBA, NFL over low IQ remarks after Celtics blockbuster trade
entry.content: Some around the league said that Jaylen Brown believes he is the smartest player in the league. On Thursday, he reiterated that sentiment while taking a jab at other athletes in the pro world.

entry.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani talks immigration, Trump administration policies in America 250 speech
entry.content: Mayor Zohran Mamdani gave a speech sitting behind the George Washington desk at City Hall on Friday morning to mark America's 250th birthday.

entry.title: IND A vs SL A 2nd Unofficial Test: Sai Sudharsan, Devdutt Padikkal put India A in command against Sri Lanka A
entry.content: Sai Sudharsan smashed his second consecutive century, remaining unbeaten on 104, as India A reached 247/1 against Sri Lanka A. Devdutt Padikkal also impressed with 94 not out, forming a strong 181-run partnership. This dominant batting display puts India A in a commanding position, trailing by 119 runs. Earlier, Sri Lanka A captain Sahan Arachchige scored 127 before India A's bowlers took wickets.

entry.title: Court says the U.S. can install historical panels at the site of Washington's Philadelphia home
entry.content: An appeals court ruled Friday that President Trump's administration can reinstall its interpretive panels at the site of President George Washington's home in Philadelphia.

entry.title: Liverpool on brink of deal for £75m star after 'positive' talks held
entry.content: Richard Hughes and Andoni Iraola are going to be at the centre of another rebuild for Liverpool. Their £450 million spend last summer clearly wasn't enough as they finished fifth in the Premier Leagu...

entry.title: World Cup 2026 TV schedule: How to watch every knockout game live in the UK
entry.content: The last-32 matches are all free-to-air on the BBC and ITV

entry.title: What we know about the Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding
entry.content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding day has arrived with Madison Square Garden under tight security. CBS News' Jo Ling Kent reports and Jack Irvin, associate music editor at People, joins to discuss.

entry.title: Flyers sign Ducks center Carlsson to 5-year, $90 million offer sheet; Anaheim has 7 days to match
entry.content: The Philadelphia Flyers are prepared to pay Anaheim center Leo Carlsson the highest annual salary at $18 million since the salary cap era began in 2005, now giving the Ducks seven days to match the offer. The Flyers tendered the five-year, $90 million offer sheet to Carlsson on Friday, which would require four of the Flyers first-round draft picks in each of the next four seasons as compensation. The Anaheim Ducks have seven days to exercise their right of first refusal on the player, per the NHL collective bargaining agreement.

entry.title: What is Mexico City weather forecast for England’s World Cup clash at the Azteca?
entry.content: England’s last-16 game against the co-hosts could be moved forward due to forecasted thunderstorms

entry.title: Days after ESPN trashed Raiders offseason, NFL.com has very different take
entry.content: This week ESPN put out a shockingly low grade of Raiders offseason. Now NFL.com has put out their grade. And it's wildly different.

entry.title: Ex-NFL GM calls Rams rookie Ty Simpson 'a career backup'
entry.content: This former general manager compared Ty Simpson to two of the biggest first-round draft busts in recent memory.

entry.title: Chris Gotterup contending at John Deere Classic, an event he never considered skipping
entry.content: Even though he's set to defend his title in Scotland next week, skipping the John Deere Clasic never crossed Chris Gotterup's mind.

entry.title: Swift-Kelce wedding day arrives with MSG under tight security
entry.content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to welcome around 1,000 guests at Madison Square Garden to celebrate their wedding, sources say.

entry.title: Dads of Camp Mystic flood victims on the pain of losing their daughters, pushing for safety changes
entry.content: Ryan DeWitt and Matthew Childress, the fathers of a camper and counselor who died during last year's deadly floods at Camp Mystic in Texas, spoke to CBS News' Jason Allen about the pain of losing their daughters and their mission to push for safety changes in summer camps across the U.S. to prevent other families from experiencing the same tragedy.

entry.title: Americans agree on more than we think, new public opinion data shows
entry.content: A new poll shows Americans agree on far more than we would think. Dr. Steven Kull, director of the Program for Public Consultation at the University of Maryland, joins CBS News to discuss.

entry.title: Record number of July 4th travelers expected despite higher gas prices, airfares
entry.content: AAA is projecting that 72 million Americans will either fly or drive more than 50 miles from home over this Fourth of July weekend. Robert Sinclair, senior manager for AAA, joins CBS News to discuss.

entry.title: Parade of ships sails through New York City in massive maritime gathering
entry.content: A flotilla of over 100 tall ships and navy vessels from across the world's waterways have traveled to New York Harbor in celebration of America's 250th birthday. CBS News' Lana Zak has more.

entry.title: Consider the parents caring for adult children not in work or education | Letter
entry.content: One reader was surprised by how many people responding to Annalisa Barbieri’s advice column equate successful parenting with independenceReading the comments below Annalisa Barbieri’s latest advice column online (I wish my son wanted to spend more time with me, 28 June), I was struck by how many people equated successful parenting with raising independent adult children.But life isn’t always that straightforward. There is a largely invisible generation of midlife parents still caring for adult children who remain Neets – not in employment, education or training. Many are living with neurodivergence, severe mental illness, post-Covid syndrome or chronic ill health. Continue reading...

entry.title: Deaths in France surged 30% during hottest week of record June heatwave
entry.content: Public health authority says 2,025 excess deaths probably an underestimate and that it expects toll to rise furtherThe number of deaths recorded in France surged by nearly 30% during the hottest week of the record-breaking heatwave that scorched much of Europe last month, the public health authority has said, adding that it expected the toll to rise further.Public Health France said on Friday there had been “an increase of 29.1%, corresponding to 2,025 additional deaths compared with the previous week”. It said the figure was probably an underestimate and “mortality will rise further”. Continue reading...

entry.title: Iran Begins Khamenei’s Funeral in Show of Defiance Against the U.S.
entry.content: Authorities predict 20 million people will attend six days of ceremonies to mourn the supreme leader killed in the war with Israel and the U.S.

entry.title: Don't throw away your shot to speak to an AI Alexander Hamilton at Boston's new finance museum
entry.content: Nearly a decade after leaving its former home, the Museum of American Finance is opening its new doors to the public.

entry.title: Eye Opener: Brutal heat wave drags into July Fourth holiday weekend
entry.content: More than half of the U.S. is still experiencing intense temperatures as a brutal heat wave continues into the holiday weekend. Plus, America turns 250 years old this weekend. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

entry.title: Mission launched to rescue a falling space telescope
entry.content: Katalyst Space's LINK spacecraft is designed to capture and boost NASA's Swift observatory back to a safe altitude.

entry.title: Maps show this July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
entry.content: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

entry.title: Dangerous heat wave impacting much of the U.S. ahead of Fourth of July celebrations
entry.content: An intense heat wave is sweeping across the East Coast as final preparations are underway for Fourth of July celebrations. Rob Marciano has a look at the forecast and its impact.

entry.title: Ukrainian woman named suspect in Monaco bombing
entry.content: The suspect was initially identified as a heavily built person appearing to be male, but Interpol now says it is looking for a 39-year-old Ukrainian woman.

entry.title: 40 killed when speeding, overcrowded bus tumbles into Pakistan ravine, officials say
entry.content: A speeding bus plunged from a highway into a rocky ravine in Pakistan, killing 40 people and injuring eight others in one of the deadliest road accidents in recent years, officials said.

entry.title: People awaiting hospital treatment to get three weeks’ notice under NHS England plans
entry.content: Move inspired by customer service provided by online retailers such as John Lewis and AmazonPeople waiting for hospital treatment will get three weeks’ notice of their next appointment under NHS plans inspired by the customer service provided by online shopping operators.Hospitals are being ordered to start telling everyone on their treatment waiting list at least three weeks before their operation, diagnostic test or meeting with a consultant. Continue reading...

entry.title: Just gave birth or are about to? Share your story and questions about costs
entry.content: The costs of having a baby in the U.S. can be hard to predict and budget for. We want to hear your stories and questions about how to navigate the system.

entry.title: This is how seriously a patient’s skin colour can affect the quality of medical care they receive | Devi Sridhar
entry.content: New reporting from the Guardian has shed further light on the ‘ethnicity pain gap’. This is what has to be done to close itRead more from the Guardian’s ethnicity pain gap seriesI always know someone is going to say something racist when they start a sentence with, “I’m not racist, but …” Nobody likes to think they would ever discriminate against someone based on the colour of their skin – and some people seem increasingly uncomfortable about acknowledging that such discrimination exists at all in the world. Yet we are now seeing a backlash from certain political groups against diversity initiatives, including from Kemi Badenoch who wants to do away with “DEI bureaucracy”, and Nigel Farage who promises to get rid of “woke” council roles such as those involved in increasing diversity, equity and inclusion.Whatever your political views, no one wants debates to be lost in emotion rather than based on the evidence. So it is helpful to come back to facts about race and how it affects people’s lives. And as new Guardian reporting on racial inequalities in pain relief reveals, when it comes to healthcare, the evidence is overwhelming: race and ethnicity are associated with differences in the quality of care people receive and, ultimately, in their health outcomes. Regardless of whether anyone is being racist, it is clear that some people receive worse healthcare because of their racial or ethnic background.Prof Devi Sridhar is chair of global public health at the University of EdinburghDo you have an opinion on the issues raised in this article? If you would like to submit a response of up to 300 words by email to be considered for publication in our letters section, please click here. Continue reading...

entry.title: Former USAID head grieves its closure while hoping for its future
entry.content: Former USAID administrator reflects on a year since USAID's shutdown and the new direction of US foreign aid.

entry.title: What's open and closed on July 3 and July 4? See which stores are operating.
entry.content: Major retail stores will be open on Friday, although some may have modified hours on Saturday, July 4.

entry.title: Summer Driving Season Is Here, and More Americans Than Ever Are Hitting the Road
entry.content: Prices at the pump are still elevated, but pressure is easing, leaving more money to spend on road-trip fun.

entry.title: Oregon ER doctors win a 'David and Goliath' battle against a national company
entry.content: In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.

entry.title: Tesla's Model Y L finally comes to the US with six seats and a $62,000 price tag
entry.content: Tesla has started taking orders for the Model Y Long Wheelbase in the US and Puerto Rico.

entry.title: Trump's ties to Erdogan sold him on this year's NATO summit. Turkey may win big in other ways
entry.content: U.S. President Donald Trump has berated and belittled many of the European leaders expected to attend next week’s NATO summit in Turkey but has only praise for its host, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan

entry.title: Latin Mass, but faithful to the pope: A Minnesota church shows a different way during schism crisis
entry.content: Priests at the Church of Saint Agnes in St. Paul, Minnesota, celebrate a traditional Latin Rite Mass every Sunday, even though the practice has become a symbol of cultural and political battles among some Catholics

entry.title: Man with a Tibetan flag dies after setting himself on fire in front of UN HQ
entry.content: Officials say a man holding a Tibetan flag in front of the United Nations headquarters in New York set himself on fire and died

entry.title: Egg producers will pay $3.3 million, donate 53 million eggs to settle price-fixing claims
entry.content: The U.S. Justice Department and 17 states reached settlement agreements with three major egg producers this week to resolve allegations that the companies illegally colluded for years to raise prices, including when the cost soared to record highs last year.

entry.title: ‘A genuine wildlife emergency’: everything you need to know about the arrival of H5 bird flu in Australia
entry.content: What is the virus? Where did it come from? Are humans at risk? What does it mean for the agricultural sector and wildlife? Experts answer your questionsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA deadly strain of bird flu, known as H5 bird flu, has arrived on the Australian mainland.The federal agriculture minister, Julie Collins, confirmed in late June that a brown skua – found sick in Western Australia – had died from the H5N1 virus. The skua is a wild migratory bird. Continue reading...

entry.title: Last-minute launch problem delays satellite rescue mission for NASA
entry.content: A rush rescue mission to save a NASA space telescope remains grounded, this time because of a last-minute launch problem

entry.title: World Cup red card controversy
entry.content: One of the star players on the U.S. men's soccer team received a red card and will be sidelined for Monday’s game. According to FIFA rules, there’s no way to appeal. NBC News’ Steve Patterson has more on what his coach is saying tonight.

entry.title: Hegseth Prepared a Bombshell Plan to Cut Troops in Europe. Then It Got Nixed.
entry.content: The defense secretary wanted to adjust U.S. troop levels in Europe but pulled back after his plan was floated to the White House.

entry.title: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
entry.content: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipientAs Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister.Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. Continue reading...

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The butterfly often forgets that it was once a caterpillar.
Lucky Numbers: 92, 53, 41, 87, 57, 15

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: When ironing a button-up shirt, flip it inside-out to easily iron over the button side.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: On average, 100 people choke to death on ball-point pens every year.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Ray Bradbury
entry.content: “Stuff your eyes with wonder... live as if you'd drop dead in ten seconds. See the world. It's more fantastic than any dream made or paid for in factories.” — Ray Bradbury

entry.title: 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' 4K movie review: Latest Pandora adventure a dazzling endurance test
entry.content: "Avatar: Fire and Ash -- Ultimate Collector's Edition" arrives on 4K with stunning Pandora visuals, Dolby Atmos sound, James Cameron spectacle and a massive making-of documentary.

entry.title: 125 passengers and crew hit with highly contagious stomach virus on cruise from San Francisco
entry.content: More than 120 passengers and crew members on a Princess Cruises ship that docked in San Francisco on Thursday were infected with a stomach virus while on their voyage, federal health officials said.

entry.title: Carney, Marcos deepen Canada-Philippines ties as Ottawa looks beyond U.S. trade
entry.content: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. pledged Thursday to strengthen trade, energy and security ties as Canada works to diversify its economy beyond the United States and deepen its engagement in the Indo-Pacific.

entry.title: Meta has released an app for making generative AI games
entry.content: Pocket is listed in app stores but seemingly unavailable in the US.

entry.title: Video in case against Pooh Shiesty shows him pressing for record label release, prosecutors say
entry.content: Prosecutors say a video shows rapper Pooh Shiesty pressing for his release from fellow rapper Gucci Mane's record label while an armed man blocks a door during an alleged robbery at a Texas music studio in January.

entry.title: The FBI is directing hundreds of analysts to its probe of Georgia's 2020 presidential election
entry.content: The FBI has asked its field offices across the country to dedicate more than 200 staffers to its investigation of the 2020 election in Georgia's Fulton County.

entry.title: A grand jury indicts Louisiana's attorney general in a fight over changes to the local courts
entry.content: Louisiana's Republican attorney general was indicted Thursday on criminal charges by a grand jury in New Orleans, accused of trying to intimidate local officials who fought a law enacted by GOP legislators to overhaul the local courts.

entry.title: Nevada transgender suspect accused of mass-shooting threats arraigned on 35 felony counts
entry.content: A Henderson, Nevada, transgender woman is facing dozens of felony charges after police say they found her sitting in a stolen car loaded with guns in a casino parking garage, uncovering what investigators described as a stockpile of weapons and a history of threats to carry out a mass shooting on the Las Vegas Strip.
